name: RegexForge
tagline: Describe a regex in plain English — then trust it, because the engine proves every match.
description:
RegexForge collapses the most-hated dev chore — hand-crafting and debugging a regex against messy real text — into one assistant-driven interaction inside Anna.

Paste a handful of raw log or CSV lines, say what you want in plain English ("pull the ISO timestamp, the level, and the UUID request id"), and watch RegexForge author a regex and **run it against your actual lines**. The window paints colored capture-group overlays directly on the text and fills a big match-ratio ring. Refine by talking — "some lines use a comma before the millis" — and the lone red line snaps green. One click copies a commented, named-group pattern plus ready-to-paste ripgrep / Python / JavaScript / grep snippets.

**The model proposes; the engine proves.** Every highlight and every number comes from a deterministic Python `re` engine — never the model — so a match is real, not hallucinated.

**What's inside**

- A premium, monospace "forge" window: live overlays, an animated match-ratio ring, and a per-group legend.
- `regex-forge` — a stdlib-only Executa stdio tool that compiles and runs the pattern and reports exact per-line match offsets, named-group spans, and compile errors.
- `regex-coach` — a SKILL.md that teaches Anna to author re-safe named-group patterns and to always verify before claiming a match.

**How to use**

Mention RegexForge in any chat (`#regexforge`), paste your lines, and describe the pattern. RegexForge does the rest — and hands you a regex you can paste with confidence.
_comment_bundled_executas: Executas that ship inside this app. `anna-app apps publish` publishes each one first, then substitutes its server-minted tool_id into every `bundled:<handle>` reference in manifest.json (in memory) and writes bundle/anna-tool-ids.js so the UI bundle reads the same ids. During `anna-app dev` the synthetic dev tool_id from each executa.json is used (and bundle/app.js falls back to it). The regex-coach Skill (executas/regex-coach/SKILL.md) ships as a declarative skill — its behavioral contract is also mirrored in manifest.system_prompt_addendum so it is effective at runtime; publish it with `anna-app executa publish` and add its minted id to required_executas to bundle it on the store.
